DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF METALLICUS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTSIS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. METALLICUS' REQUEST NO. 24 IS GRANTED. THE TRUST DOCUMENTS ARE POTENTIALLY RELEVANT. THE KRUGER PARTIES SHALL, WITHIN 15 DAYS SERVE ALL PROMISED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS NOT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED. NO SANCTIONS. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER TRACKING THE COURT'S RULING AND IS TO INCLUDE A PROVISION FOR "ATTORNEYS' EYES ONLY" ADDED TO THE PROTECTIVE ORDER AND EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2024 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jul-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Bakari E. Ziegler, Esq., MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP, 415-442-1000, bakari.ziegler@morganlewis.com for Plaintiff METALLICUS, INC. and Cross-Defendants MARSHALL HAYNER, IRINA BERKON, and SYED JAFRI.
Derek Linke, Esq., Newman Du Wors LLP., 459-944-5422, linke@newmanlaw.com, for Defendants Frederick Krueger, SoftAtom, Inc., and Fred Krueger Charitable Remainder Trust.

All parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen hearing today's motion and authorization is given for the clerk sign the stipualtion on their behalf.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as modified in court:

PLAINTIFF METALLICUS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents

The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows:

The responses to the Requests for Production were signed under penalty of perjury. The court is unable, on this evidentiary record, to determine whether certain documents were withheld and must, instead, rely on the verifications supporting the statements that all responsive documents would be produced or that no responsive documents could be located.

Where it is alleged that no responsive documents exist, the Kruger parties shall, within 15 days, amend their responses to explain why they cannot produce the sought-after documents, stating that the documents (1) never existed; (2) were destroyed, lost, misplaced, stolen, or (3) never in the respondent's possession. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2031.230.

Metallicus' Request No. 24 is granted. The trust documents are potentially relevant.

The Kruger parties shall, within 15 days serve all promised responsive documents not previously produced.

No sanctions. A request for sanctions, the amount of the sanctions, and the identity of the party subject to those sanctions must be located in the notice of motion. CCP 2023.040.

Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare a proposed order tracking the court's ruling and is to include a provision for "Attorneys' Eyes Only" added to the Protective Order and email to Judge Pro Tem Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
